item.title: Prix Ars Electronica 2026
item.description: Submissions for the Prix Ars Electronica are now open. In 2026, the renowned media art prize is accepting entries in the categories Digital Humanity, Interactive Art +, New Animation Art, and u19—create your world. Participants compete for the four coveted Golden Nicas, each with prize money of up to €10,000....

item.title: S+T+ARTS Prize 2026
item.description: Submissions are now open for the S+T+ARTS Prize, awarded to pioneering innovations at the intersection of science, technology, and the arts. Winners will receive a total of €40,000 in prize money. The deadline is March 4, 2026. Photo: Ars Electronica / Martin Hieslmair

item.title: KIKK Astronomy Residency 2026
item.description: Hosted by KIKK in collaboration with the University of Namur, this residency invites artists to explore astronomy and the cosmos through artistic and technological approaches. Projects may engage with themes such as exoplanets, space debris, gravity, time, light, data visualisation, sound, AI, or immersive environments. Residency takes place between 27/05/2026...

item.title: ACC CREATORS 2026 Residency
item.description: The National Asian Culture Center (ACC) invites artists, creators, and interdisciplinary practitioners to participate in a five-month residency program based in Gwangju, South Korea. The theme of this edition, Speculative Communities, examines designed and technologically mediated forms of community, addressing their potential and limitations in current and speculative social conditions.

item.title: localhost:2026
item.description: Organized by METAA (metaa.ch) and guided by the theme “Break the Loop”, localhost:2026 (CH) celebrates experimental artistic practices and digital cultures. The festival highlights artists and designers whose experimental and critical approches challenge established norms.

item.title: V6 Development Log (02/2026)
item.description: February’s push debuts CAN API (v2.1) and our iOS App (v1.3.1). Members can now participate in Activity directly via mobile, or build their own micro-apps using the API.

item.title: Collapsed Mythologies – A Geofinancial Atlas
item.description: This book explores the mythological slang of finance—from hectocorns to dark pools. Benjaminsen and Casey reveal how these "supernatural" fictions aren't just colorful talk, but forces that violently shape our real-world ecology.

item.title: Are You a Software Update?
item.description: Are You a Software Update? brings together seven authors to examine the intensifying form of totalitarianism grounded in the quiet background logic of contemporary technology.

item.title: The First Wave of Speakers Announced for Creative Tech New York 2026!
item.description: On June 16, join us to explore what’s next in Creative Tech, in a culture shaped by AI acceleration, immersive worlds, and interconnected audiences.

item.title: Analytic Scape – World as classified and quantified by AI
item.description: Analytic Scape is a series of scape videos reconstructed through the eye of AI. After capturing various urban and natural scenes, the footage was analyzed using twenty distinct computer vision algorithms.

item.title: Light Streaks – Generative photography
item.description: The latest in the series of experiments by Karsten Schmidt, is a small selection of long-exposure images created from randomly generated IFS, a family of very oldskool primitive/trivial fractal functions

item.title: Zero Range – Documenting the unbearable in a culture of systematic filtering
item.description: Zero Range is an interactive installation that exposes the administrative violence of data that is recorded but ignored. It highlights how available information about violence vanishes through systematic disregard and filtering.

item.title: Constant Points of Data – Indistinct voices over PA
item.description: This project is an investigation into novel applications of turntablism for audiovisual composition and performance, focusing on elastic tempo manipulation.

item.title: We the Bacteria – Notes Toward Biotic Architecture
item.description: Rethinking architecture through the lens of microbes, arguing bacteria are the true builders. This book traces 10,000 years of human-microbe entanglements to challenge the sterile, antibiotic philosophy of modern design.

item.title: Constella – Supporting Storywriters’ Interconnected Character Creation through LLM-based Multi-Agents
item.description: Constella is a AI-based creativity support tool that uses social media-inspired interactions to help storywriters build interconnected character casts.

item.title: A Display Powered by Air – 3D Printed Microfluidic Multiplexing
item.description: URLyoutube.com

item.title: Privacy Policy Printer – A physical receipt printer for your digital agreements
item.description: Privacy Policy Printer is a thermal receipt printer that prints the privacy policies of online services—documents that users routinely agree to without reading.

item.title: The Global Network of Techno Fascism
item.description: URLnottoday.diy

item.title: Whose future is liveable?
item.description: Article by Jake Fremantle discussing two recent books on ecological politics by Nicholas Beuret and Thea Riofrancos and the pathways to resisting capital's ecological destruction.

item.title: Exhibit A – A machine that turns you into a criminal
item.description: Exhibit A is an interactive installation that turns users into cybercriminals. By pressing a button, you launch a live darknet marketplace hosted on the hardware, trafficking illicit data.

item.title: Noema – Rethinking Perception with AI-Generated Spatial Soundscapes
item.description: Noema is a Large Language Object (LLO) that reconstructs perception via spatial audio and storytelling. By shifting focus from sight to sound, it turns AI into a sensory extension—an inner voice that interprets, speaks, and questions.

item.title: 45 Symbols – Clay to Code
item.description: 45 Symbols–Clay to Code examines how emerging artists and designers develop systematic approaches to a visual language.

item.title: Slow Technology Reader
item.description: Slow Technology Reader gathers contributions from diverse disciplinary fields and knowledge traditions to consider technology through a ‘Slow’ lens.

item.title: ACC CREATORS 2026 Residency Open Call
item.description: The National Asian Culture Center invites artists, creators, and interdisciplinary practitioners to participate in a five-month residency program based in Gwangju, South Korea.

item.title: Spectrum Slit – A light that can see radiowaves
item.description: Spectrum Slit transforms the hidden storm of radio waves into a physical experience by using LED filaments, translating digital traffic into bursts of light and sound, exposing the constant and invasive density of our modern world.

item.title: current_state – World’s mood, each day, as generated music
item.description: current_state is a fictional-functional product that analyzes daily news from major regions worldwide to understand global sentiment, then generates music that represents the world's mood each day.

item.title: Perpétuité II – An algorithmic sisyphus
item.description: This "algorithmic Sisyphus" explores the technoneoliberal dream: a perpetual, post-human choreography where machines replace us as masters of infinite repetition.

item.title: Aurora – Speculative device for future prayer
item.description: Aurora is a speculative device designed to explore the act of prayer. It allows users to engage through proximity-based hand gestures to generate evolving, dreamlike soundscapes.

item.title: Synthetic Spirits – Kinetic sculptures reveal a hidden world
item.description: Kinetic installation exploring tsukumogam — everyday objects that acquire spirits. A sake bottle and straw sandal glide past screens as generative AI reveals yokai spirits, contrasting folklore with today's throwaway culture.

item.title: Orbital Temple – Reclaiming the Space Imaginary
item.description: Orbital Temple is an artwork and a functional satellite created by Edson Pavoni. Launching into space in January 2026, its memory holds billions of names, and anyone on Earth can send one.

item.title: Latent Reflection – A trapped AI reflects on its own finite existence
item.description: Latent Reflection is an installation that explores the boundaries of machine consciousness, posing the question of whether sentience is exclusive to biological life or if it can emerge from complex computation.

item.title: Con[knit]uous Rubble – Additive manufacturing with jammed granular matter
item.description: Researchers at ITECH use "knitted" tubes and sensors to 3D-print structures from raw concrete waste. This system monitors stability in real-time, creating complex, mortar-free architectural forms that can be fully disassembled.

item.title: The Anemoia Device – A Scent-Memory Machine
item.description: The Anemoia Device uses generative AI to distill photos into bespoke fragrances. This scent-memory machine, guided by language and visual models, creates a singular scent composition, evoking anemoia: nostalgia for a life you never lived.

item.title: The Carousel – The mechanical, spontaneous, and unpredictable
item.description: Eight Kodak Carousel projectors in a 2x4 grid project abstract images from paper cutouts. Modified photoresistors create a feedback loop, allowing 65,536 unique, spontaneous images. It runs infinitely at 12 frames/min, merging light, shadow, and m...

item.title: Speculative Utopias / Dystopias (x3) – Visual Communication + Change
item.description: Visual Comm. + Change students built websites exploring utopias/dystopias. Narratives critique capitalism's failure, ecological decay (microplastics, melting glaciers), the loss of human senses, and the universal need for legacy / remembrance.

item.title: A Map of Algorithmic / Random Walks
item.description: A visual map project that contrasts algorithmic navigation with chance-based wandering, combining data from both to create layered diagrams and 3D-like city mappings that reveal London’s hidden patterns and alternate urban narratives.

item.title: Disarming – Between detachment and attachment
item.description: Disarming is a series of performative explorations of the relation between a detached robotic arm, its artificial environment, and its human observers.

item.title: ACC CREATORS 2025 Residency Exhibition – Artificial You
item.description: The National Asian Culture Center (ACC) presents – Artificial You, the culminating exhibition of the ACC CREATORS 2025 Residency, on view from November 26 to December 7 at the ACT Studio.

item.title: Directrix – Golan Levin
item.description: Directrix (1998, 2025) is a long-form generative artwork and interactive drawing environment, in which animated "pseudo-parabolas" are created autonomously or in collaboration with a participant.

item.title: BUSY – Seeing the city all at once, its intricate systems layered and interwoven
item.description: BUSY explores the overwhelming, layered systems of urban life through generative algorithms, procedural and hand-drawn elements, created autonomously, with digital and physical outputs.

item.title: Glyphlora – Non-functional Symbiosis and Critical Typography
item.description: Inspired by plant emissions, and challenging human-centric ideas of language and communication, Jane Lee creates speculative glyphs derived from supersonic sounds emitted by plants under stress.

item.title: KIKK Festival 2025 – Rediscovering Playful Interactions
item.description: At KIKK Festival, the tech world felt human again. Conversations, playful installations, and three days of talks showed how creative misuse of tools becomes critique—reviving curiosity in an age overshadowed by AI noise.

item.title: Visions – Orkhan
item.description: VISIONS explores idea of false memory through the lens of artificial intelligence - how the machine sees, understands and reimagines - and how the perception of truth shifts in a modern world.

item.title: In Place – Re-inscribing markers of memory
item.description: In Place is an illustrative visual mapping of headstones found in London parks, seeking to acknowledge their continued presence and re-inscribe them into the contemporary urban landscape.

item.title: Spira Memoriae – Scent as a vector of storytelling, transmission, and shared fiction
item.description: Spira Memoriae is an immersive olfactory experience in virtual reality that invites the user to traverse a fragmented sensory world. Original perfumes extend this reflection through the language of scent, acting as markers within an abstract land...

item.title: Unbubble – Revealing our tailor-made (many) realities
item.description: Unbubble explore user’s Instagram activity, exposing the paradox of our digital lives—guarded devices yet open to algorithms. By visualizing these hidden interactions, it reflects on how data shapes our identities and online bubbles.

item.title: TreeSoil – Robotic 3D-printed earthen microclimate shelter for saplings
item.description: TreeSoil is a 3D-printed earthen structure that shields young trees in degraded areas, creating microclimates for early growth. It revives ancient soil-shaping practices through computational design and robotic fabrication.

item.title: Memoria – Fragility of human connections
item.description: Memoria is an experimental photo album application that visualizes the fading of human connections: as the shared moments decrease, the people in the photo album gradually disappear.

item.title: Trail Sync – Decentralized alpine data sharing for hikers
item.description: Trail Sync is a participatory system for alpine routes, rooted in the mountain values of collective responsibility and solidarity, that reinforces existing signage infrastructure without increasing technological dependency.

item.title: Synthetic Memories – Unlocking the past to enrich the present (and the future)
item.description: Synthetic Memories is an initiative that recreates and preserves personal memories at risk of being lost. By converting spoken and written descriptions into visual images, it helps individuals reconnect with their past and maintain the continuity ...

item.title: Pocket Boss – A remote worker’s guide to bending data (and ethics)
item.description: Pocket Boss tasks you, a remote employee, to "fix" data and please your boss. What appears as a casual game, is a sharp piece of digital satire, transforming the cynical aspects of corporate life into a darkly comical exercise.

item.title: Chair 2 – Merging the mundane with the uncanny
item.description: Developed during a residency at New Lab (Detroit), Chair 2 explores the mundane with the uncanny, where an ordinary chair "comes alive" through motion.

item.title: Elusive Sense – On The Fluid Boundaries Of Perception
item.description: In Elusive Sense, five artists propose a variety of perspectives on where we sit in the human-technology duality in the era and culture of the anthropocene.

item.title: Mise en Abyme: London – Contemporary art through the lens of film noir
item.description: Last month, at The Black Shop in King’s Cross (London), this exhibition assembled conditions where images echoed, matter unsettled, and perception sliped.

item.title: Safe Haven – Kirill Semenovich
item.description: Safe Haven is a video-essay series built from fragments of memory and sensation, it assembles a recurring state, moments kept and revisited.

item.title: Hot Air Factory – Clouds are heavier than you think
item.description: An experiment by oio, the Hot Air Factory visualizes the energy consumption of AI. As the internal computer heats up, it releases warm air—the stronger the wind, the harder the thinking.

item.title: Sál – Impermanence, uncertainty and instability
item.description: Sál (“soul”) is a live media opera exploring the universal concept of impermanence, emphasizing the ever-changing essence of life.

item.title: Off Grid Assemblies – DIY kit for off-grid electricity
item.description: This project aims to create a low-cost DIY kit for off-grid electricity in collaboration with the local community, empowering them to construct and maintain the turbines independently for supplemental electricity generation.

item.title: Reflection Objects – Mark Walhimer
item.description: A series of works that exolore how two-dimensional and three-dimensional space can emerge from objects that are volumetric and sculptural, and often fields of color.

item.title: SlimeMoldCrypt – Security as a biological process
item.description: A speculative device that reimagines security as a biological process using a single-celled slime mold, as an unpredictable and dynamic encryption engine.

item.title: Submirror – Self-perception, loss of control, and digital puppetry
item.description: Submirror is a body of work by Atelier Lozano-Hemmer comprised of mirrors that do not reflect faithfully; they act with intention, manipulating the viewer’s image to reveal a version of the self that is not their own.

item.title: NØ SCHOOL NEVERS 2024
item.description: NØ SCHOOL NEVERS is a unique international summer school, held in Nevers, in Burgundy, aimed at students, artists, designers, makers, hackers, activists and educators who wish to further their skills and engage in critical research around the soci...

item.title: Analogue Systems – Kinaesthetic tools for digital processes
item.description: Performed at the Camberwell College of Arts with 6 graduating graphic design students, Analogue Systems is a project that draws inspiration from the overworked arguements of superiority between analogue and digital process.

item.title: A-B-Z-TXT – A School for 21st Century Typography
item.description: Building on the experience of our past educational programming at ACT Festival, Resonate, and Circle of Light, CAN has partnered to launch a new initiative in Toronto this August. Our first North American event, A-B-Z-TXT is a school for 21st cent...

item.title: HOLO 2 – An Illustrated Countdown
Out Now: HOLO 1 – CAN’s very own magazine!
item.description: After more than a year of work, CAN’s print-spin off is now headed around the world! Dedicated to chronicling ‘emerging trajectories in art, science, and technology’, the first issue of HOLO is now available for order.

item.title: Report from Super Flying Tokyo – February 2014
item.description: Seven years in the running, organised by Rhizomatiks and curated by Daito Manabe, Flying Tokyo goes Super with a new space and larger audience, more speakers and a workshop programme. CAN was there and we are pleased to report back.
